title: SEC fines Citi's investment arm $2.9 million in recordkeeping case
text: The Securities and Exchange Commission said late Tuesday it has settled cease-and-desist proceedings against Citigroup Inc.'s C, +0.97% broker-dealer unit Citigroup Global Markets Inc. “for willfully violating recordkeeping requirements” regarding expenses related to its underwriting business from 2009 through May 2019. “CGMI used an unsubstantiated and unverified method to calculate and record indirect expenses associated with its work as an underwriter,” the SEC said.

title: The Buffett List: 6 Dividend Buys In August
text: This Buffett holdings list from Kiplinger first appeared 8/21/23 online. Kiplinger, YCharts and Dogs of The Dow all track this Buffett/Berkshire batch. Here is your update from 8/22/23 YCharts. 32 of 48 current Berkshire Hathaway-owned stocks pay dividends. As of 8/22/23, the top-ten ranged 2.87%-5.02% by annual yield and 45 of 48 ranged -0.16%-57.71% per broker-estimated target-price-upsides. Analyst-augured top-ten net gains from Buffett's August dividend dogs ranged 19.12%-56.48% from CVX, COF, DHI, LPX, KHC, BAC, PARA, ALLY, C, and top pick GM per YCharts data.

title: Citigroup Stock Is Trading Below Its Intrinsic Value
text: Citigroup's stock (NYSE: C) has lost 9% YTD, as compared to the 14% rise in the S&P500 over the same period.

title: 3 Dirt-Cheap Stocks That Could Skyrocket
text: Investors worried about headwinds overshot their target with most bank stocks, and Citigroup is no exception. Expedia shares tumbled on worries that demand for travel services isn't quite as firm as believed.

title: Judge rules syndicated loan was not a security in victory for JPMorgan Chase and three other banks
text: JPMorgan Chase & Co. JPM, +0.07% and three other banks on Thursday won an appeal in U.S. District Court for the Southern District of New York that held that a $1.78 billion leveraged loan extended in 2014 to urine testing company Millennium Laboratories does not fit the legal definition of a security. In a decision with positive implications for bank and private credit lenders, Circuit Judge Jose A.

title: Citi Makes Strategic Investment in Rextie, Peru's Leading Fintech for FX Services
text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Citi, a global leader in foreign exchange (FX) markets, has made a strategic investment in Rextie, Peru's leading fintech for FX services. The investment makes Rextie Latin America's first FX fintech to receive an investment from one of the largest banks in the world. Citi's cutting-edge FX technology will also be integrated into Rextie's currency exchange services. Through CitiFX Pulse and Instant Payments, Rextie's clients will experience a service that stands out f.

title: Citi mulling further reorganization under CEO Jane Fraser: reports
text: The reorganization train continues to roll at Citigroup Inc., with further changes in the works under CEO Jane Fraser, according to reports.

title: Citigroup eyes more fee cuts in ‘coming months'
text: Citigroup Inc. C, -2.50% said Wednesday it plans to cut additional fees in coming months after eliminating overdraft fees, returned item fees and overdraft protection fees starting in 2022. The bank is also launching a relationship tiers program, which the bank describes as similar to an airline or hotel rewards program.

title: Better.Com planning IPO via reverse merger
text: A company that gained notoriety when the CEO canned 900 staff via Zoom is seeking a stock market listing. Online mortgage originator Better.Com is planning to go public via Aurora Acquisition Corp, a SPAC (special-purpose acquisition company).

title: Citi Simplifies Retail Banking to Help Customers Achieve Their Financial Potential
text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Citi's U.S. Retail Bank introduces simplified banking, a more straightforward way of banking. By retiring account packages and introducing Relationship Tiers, Citi is streamlining its benefits and services and enhancing the customer experience. New customers can benefit from these changes immediately and Citi will begin converting existing customers to simplified banking in 2024.

title: Stock Market Crash Alert: S&P Bank Downgrades Rock Stocks
text: A number of major American banks are in the red today following S&P Globals' credit ratings adjustments on several regional lenders yesterday. Fears of a stock market crash are swirling as equity markets continue to show some sheepishness heading further into the second half of the year.

title: U.S. banks and regional lenders slide across the board as S&P is latest to downgrade ratings
text: U.S. banks and regional banks fell across the board on Tuesday, after S&P Global Ratings downgraded five smaller players after a review of risk related to funding, liquidity and asset quality with a focus on office commercial real estate.

title: Is Sea's Recent Slump Warranted?
text: Singapore-based technology company Sea Ltd. ( SE , Financial) faces significant headwinds amid various downgrades from Wall Street analysts following its second-quarter earnings results.

title: 3 Major Regional Banks to Watch Despite High Rates, Waning Loans
text: A steady rise in funding costs due to higher rates, waning loan demand, poor asset quality and probable economic downturn are likely to hurt Zacks Major Regional Banks' financials. However, industry players like JPM, BAC & C are worth watching on restructuring and digitization efforts.

title: Citi Survey Shows Challenges Ahead from Accelerated Settlements
text: LONDON & NEW YORK & HONG KONG--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Citi today launched the third edition of its “Securities Services Evolution” whitepaper series, which shows the securities ecosystem faces challenging times ahead. The whitepaper finds that accelerated settlement is the single largest area of focus across all financial market infrastructures (FMIs) and market participants globally, with 77% of respondents expecting a major impact on their business. “Our research shows that the rapidly accelerating.

title: Citigroup reportedly mulling big shake-up that would give CEO more power
text: Citigroup Inc (NYSE:C)'s chief executive, Jane Fraser, is contemplating a significant restructuring of the bank's primary division, marking the most substantial reorganization in nearly 15 years, according to the Financial Times. The proposal involves dividing the Institutional Clients Group (ICG) — responsible for almost three-quarters of Citi's $14.8bn net profits in 2022 — into its three main sectors: investment and corporate banking, global markets, and transaction services.

title: The 3 Most Undervalued Warren Buffett Stocks to Buy Now: August 2023
text: You can (usually) never go wrong aligning your portfolio with legendary investors, which brings us to undervalued Warren Buffett stocks. To be quite blunt, not everyone trusts the top holdings of the Oracle of Omaha's conglomerate firm Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE: BKR-B ).

title: House GOP subpoenas Citibank over alleged Jan. 6 'back-channel' cooperation with the FBI
text: House Judiciary Committee Chairman Jim Jordan has issued a subpoena to Citibank as part of a hunt for information on whether banks shared private customer data with the FBI after the January 6 insurrection.

title: Goldman Sachs still a buy, bank analyst says, despite noise around CEO David Solomon
text: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. Chief Executive David Solomon should stay in the job despite recent criticism and even as the bank's stock performance has lagged its peers, Odeon Capital analyst Dick Bove said on Friday.

title: Bank of America, JPMorgan and four other financial stocks recommended by Oppenheimer as ‘undervalued'
text: Bank of America Corp. BAC, -0.03%, JPMorgan Chase & Co. JPM, -1.04%, Citigroup C, -0.42%, Morgan Stanley MS, -0.23%, Goldman Sachs Group Inc. GS, -0.48% and U.S. Bancorp USB, -0.65% are being recommended by Oppenheimer as undervalued to the market, the firm said in a research note on Friday. The KBW Nasdaq Bank Index BKX, -0.02% fell about 30% relative to the market in the weeks immediately after the collapse of Silicon Valley Bank in March, even though the industry's fundamentals have remained stable in the ensuing months, Oppenheimer analysts said.

title: Are Major U.S. Banks At Risk Of Credit-Ratings Downgrades?
text: Shares of the biggest banks in the U.S., including JPMorgan Chase & Co. NYSE: JPM, Bank of America NYSE: BAC, Citigroup Inc. NYSE: C, Wells Fargo & Co. NYSE: WFC and Morgan Stanley NYSE: MS traded lower on August 16, continuing sector declines that began earlier in the week.

title: Are You Missing Out on This Bank's Monster Dividend Raise?
text: One of the U.S.'s Big Four lenders hiked its quarterly payout by almost 17%. That's a much higher bump than the declared and intended raises of the other three.

title: Jim Jordan subpoenas Citibank over alleged info sharing with FBI
text: A GOP-led congressional subcommittee subpoenaed Citibank Thursday over potential information sharing with the FBI.

title: House Republicans subpoena Citibank over info shared with FBI after Jan. 6
text: The subpoena comes after a voluntary request to several financial institutions for the information.

title: 7 Hidden-Gem Stocks to Buy Before the Next Big Wave
text: As the stock market is showing an unexpected pattern, investors are on the hunt for hidden-gem stocks that hold the potential to break free from the pack and deliver substantial gains. Amidst the noise of popular market giants, a select group of under-the-radar companies is quietly positioning themselves for exceptional growth in the coming years.

title: Fitch warns multiple US banks face credit downgrade: report
text: A Fitch Ratings analyst warned the agency may be forced to downgrade dozens of U.S. banks, including major lenders like JPMorgan Chase and Bank of America.

title: Fitch warns it may have to downgrade slew of US banks including giants like JPMorgan
text: Fitch Ratings in June cut its "operating environment" score for the US banking sector. Fitch analyst Chris Wolfe told CNBC that another downgrade would mean banking giants like JPMorgan face reevaluations.

title: U.S. bank stocks drop after Fitch downgrade warning
text: Shares of U.S. banks dropped in premarket trading after a Fitch Ratings analyst told CNBC that the agency could downgrade several lenders, while a proposed rule change by a top banking regulator added to investor worries.

title: Big US banks are reportedly trying to dump commercial real estate loans - but buyers are scarce as pressures mount for property markets
text: Big banks are trying to dump commercial real estate loans as pressures mount in the sector.  JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, and Capital One are among those trying to shed debt exposure, sources told Bloomberg.

title: Citigroup Offers Compelling Value
text: Citigroup Inc. ( C , Financial), a New York-based global financial services company, offers a wide array of services encompassing consumer banking, credit services, corporate and investment banking, securities brokerage, trade and securities services and wealth management.

title: Oil & Gas Are Moving In August, Here Are The 3 Industry Favorites
text: Whether it is the forces of commodity cycles, the changing tides of a world economy, or even straight manipulation from the most prominent players who hear whispers from OPEC's next decision, the only certainty is that oil and gas prices are swinging hard. Crude oil has swung from below $60 per barrel in the last quarter to above $80 per barrel as of today's new quarter, and natural gas has followed a similar path.

title: Moody's Slashes Ratings on Top US Banks: 5 ‘Strong Buy' Dividend Giants Are the Only Safe Plays Now
text: They assured us after the failure of Silicon Valley Bank, First Republic and Signature Bank that all was well, that the banking system was totally secure and in no danger of further failures.

title: Markets Plunge at Midday After Bank Downgrades
text: U.S. equities sank at midday on Tuesday, Aug. 8, 2023, following bank downgrades by Moody's and data showing credit card debt rose to a record.

title: Why Are Bank Stocks Down Today?
text: Bank stocks are falling on Tuesday and investors can look to Moody's lowering credit ratings as the reason for the decline. The credit rating business started off the bad news for bank stocks by cutting credit ratings for 10 banks.

title: Citigroup, Wells Fargo, PNC: Undervalued Dividend Growers
text: If you're exploring dividend stocks, you can adopt various strategies. You might seek out high-yield stocks, or those with consistent payouts and robust financial conditions, or companies with a track record of boosting dividends.

title: Moody's warns it could cut credit ratings of 6 big US banks
text: Moody's has put the credit ratings of six large US banks, including Bank of New York Mellon, State Street and Northern Trust, under review for a possible downgrade.

title: Citigroup: Bull Thesis Delayed But Not Aborted
text: Citigroup's bull thesis is still expected to play out, but timelines have been pushed back to the end of 2025 due to recent setbacks. Unfortunately, the key risks of higher capital requirements and a delay in the sale of Banamex Mexico have played out. The good news, these are only temporary setbacks and the patient investor is likely to be rewarded handsomely.

title: Citigroup's Asia family office clients poised to grow 25% in 2023
text: Citigroup's Asia family office business expects a 25% increase in clients this year, the U.S. bank's regional head of its family office advisory business said, as Singapore and Hong Kong vie to attract more wealth.

title: Bruce Berkowitz Cuts Back on Top Holding St. Joe as Shares Rise
text: Bruce Berkowitz (Trades, Portfolio), leader of Fairholme Capital Management, revealed earlier this week he curbed his investment in The St. Joe Co. ( JOE , Financial) by 3.28%.

title: Citigroup CEO of institutional clients group Paco Ybarra to leave - memo
text: Paco Ybarra, the CEO of Citigroup's institutional clients group, will leave in the first half of next year, according to an internal memo seen by Reuters.

title: JPMorgan, Goldman and Morgan Stanley win praise in Jefferies Q2 bank earnings wrap
text: Bank fundamentals were challenged but results were better than worst-case fears as higher interest rates boosted bank loan yields, Jefferies analyst Ken Usdin said Thursday in the firm's summary of second-quarter profits for the 36 large and mid-sized banks in its coverage universe.

title: Why Investors Should Be Loving C.F. Industries This Summer
text: As the United States economy continues to show signs of a potential contraction, especially after following the nine-month consecutive contracting readings in the ISM manufacturing PMI index, some industries are beginning to show signs of favoritism. As the cycle gets underway, markets usually switch their preferences between the industries deemed 'cyclical' in and / or out of those opposed as 'defensives.

title: Sell Alert: 3 Financial Services Stocks to Dump ASAP
text: It's been a tough year for banks and the financial services industry, primarily from the implosion of several regional lenders that resulted in government takeovers. Year to date, the S&P Banks Select Industry Index is down 7%.
